item.title: On this day in 2003, American activist Rachel Corrie was brutally murdered by Israelis while trying to defend a Palestinian home from demolition. They killed her by driving a bulldozer over her and the home she was defending
item.description: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rachel_Corrie "Still wearing her fluorescent jacket, she sat down at least 15 meters in front of the bulldozer, and began waving her arms and shouting, just as activists had successfully done dozens of times that day. The bulldozer continued driving forward headed straight for Rachel. When it got so close that it was moving the earth beneath her, she climbed onto the pile of rubble being pushed by the bulldozer. She got so high onto it that she was at eye-level with the cab of the bulldozer. Her head and upper torso were above the bulldozer’s blade, and the bulldozer driver and co-operator could clearly see her. Despite this, he continued forward, which pulled her legs into the pile of rubble, and pulled her down out of view of the driver. If he'd stopped at this point, he may have only broken her legs, but he continued forward, which pulled her underneath the bulldozer. We ran towards him, and waved our arms and shouted, one activist with a megaphone. But the bulldozer driver continued forward, until Rachel was underneath the cab of the bulldozer." -Joe Carr, fellow activist You don't hate Israel enough.
item.dc:creator: Freyja33

item.title: Christian Bale's $22M foster care village just outside LA in Palmdale is almost complete. With 11 residential buildings already up and completion expected later this year.
item.description: The project, developed by Bale’s nonprofit Together California alongside Dr. Eric Esrailian, is designed to keep siblings in foster care from being separated, pairing them with full-time, professionally trained foster parents in a purpose-built community. The village will also include a 7,000-square-foot community center, enrichment programs and green space, temporary housing for birth parents and transitional apartments for teens aging out of care. LA County has more children in foster care than anywhere in the U.S. Bale says this is the work he’ll be most proud of when he looks back on his life.
item.dc:creator: kenistod

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @MONGOMAKE made these cute coasters. I love sunflowers. Great job.
27 Man Tax: Ian Somerhalder
“Whatever creativity is, it is in part a solution to a problem.” - Brian Aldiss So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We also have Jack Box game night and are setting up movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ I'm also on the imgcat server for updates & fun: https://discord.gg/TpjD37jgJ9 https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Unable to work, zero income. Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, y'all are the bee's knees. You've honestly been my lifeline. (Apparently doesn't work in firefox). ***Tag me if you see an artist/creator you like whose work isn't getting seen as much as it should be! Or if you are an artist/creator and want me to post something of yours, DM me
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: But wait, there’s more!
item.description: It looks like wealth inequality declines among seniors, but this is partly due to the early deaths of the poorest people. “While wealth inequality narrows after 65 across the U.S. and Europe, in the U.S. it narrows because the poorest Americans die sooner and in greater proportion,” Papanicolas said. Sauce: https://www.powershealth.org/about-us/newsroom/health-library/2025/04/03/even-wealthy-americans-die-younger-than-europeans
item.dc:creator: RipThemUpRatchet

item.title: God Shred The King.
item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: My Immortal
item.description: For some reason, I always thought kidney disease could be cured. I was wrong. A year ago I was diagnosed with stage 2 kidney disease. I wasn't told by the doctor that it wasn't curable. I thought, well if I change all of my habits, It can heal it's self, right? I put off going back to the doctor for further check up because of family and financial reasons. Last week I finally went back in for a checkup. It's gotten worse. I'm really close to stage 3 kidney disease. So now I have to change my lifestyle again. In a panic, I googled what I could do to slow the process. I'm 54 and have been smoking since I was 16. I quit smoking 2 days ago. I'm doing everything I possibly can to slow this even more. I'm currently trying to get a referral from my doctor for a nephrologist. I was told if I give up and let things run it's course, I might live to 60. If I fight like hell, I might live to 70. I was really hoping for one last shot at falling in love and spending my final years with someone. It looks like that is a no go because who in their right mind would want to fall in love with someone knowing that they may only have a few years to spend with that person. Thank you if you read this. I needed to tell someone. Edit: Thanks all. I've been faced with my mortality before, but this one hit hard.
item.dc:creator: TheDiscoVampire

item.title: How in the hell do Republicans continue to stand behind this filth?
item.description:
https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2026/03/16/trump-neal-dunn-terminal-illness/
This is wild. It feels low, even for Trump. And that's saying A LOT. Dude just told the whole world this congressman is dying. This might be how friends and family find out. I've given up hope that Republicans, whether it's voters or elected officials, will ever break away from this disgusting individual, but I still can't help but wonder why when something like this happens.
item.dc:creator: Illinifan88

item.title: "Hey, 'Epstein class'! We are coming for you!"
item.description: Philadelphia District Attorney Larry Krasner: 'Hey, "Epstein class." You may think that whatever happened on that island or happened in Epstein's mansion is not going to haunt you! Let me tell you who is going to haunt you: It's the prosecutors at the state court level who still care about the Constitution, the laws, and justice; justice for everyone, including victims. We are a problem for you because there are things that some of you did in our local jurisdictions that violate state laws. We are coming for you. The period of time we have to bring these charges is very long because we're talking about the potential victimization of children! And when we come for you, and if we are able to convict you, the sentence you get, you will serve! Because you cannot be pardoned by this or any other president! The project to fight against federal overreach — the organization of state court prosecutors and their allies, who are ready to make sure there is accountability when it comes to federal officers committing crimes in their jurisdictions: we're also ready to make sure that there's accountability when it comes to Epstein!'
item.dc:creator: originalmaja

item.title: This dude is a pedophile criminal idiot sundowning, crashing out. Now he's saying like 4 psycho contradicting things in single sentences, & for-profit billionaire media is still sanewashing and crafting narratives... but when does it all fall apart?
item.description:
Some late night redditor is going to gasp at this one.
I actually don't get this one.
Europeans when America insults them, slaps illegal tariffs on them, starts a dumbass needless illegal war with ZERO plan, in fact, less of a plan in ignoring 50 years of strategy and planning... and now he's asking them for help after saying he didn't need it a week ago? This guy is sundowning so rapidly.

item.title: Tulip Mania?
item.description: Goldman Sachs Chief Economist Jan Hatzius, said in an interview with the Atlantic Council that AI investment spending has had “basically zero” contribution to the U.S. GDP growth in 2025. “We don’t actually view AI investment as strongly growth positive,” said Hatzius. “I think there’s a lot of misreporting, actually, of the impact AI investment had on U.S. GDP growth in 2025, and it’s much smaller than is often perceived.” Hatzius said one major reason is that much of the equipment powering AI is imported. On top of that, there is currently no reliable way to accurately measure how AI use among businesses and consumers contributes to economic growth.
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: I have an escape plan
item.description: Im in the safest place in the world, I feel. But if I need to run i have fail safes, solutions to potential problems, and places run and hide. Im building a bug out bag. item.title: Going to be a difficult few days....
item.description:
We, as a family, had know my grandmother wasn't going to be around much longer. Her health had nose dived in the last year, couple of falls and other maladies. So we were prepared, funeral was already arranged and even paid for. I got to say good bye over the phone Thursday, glad I made that call now. I should consider myself very lucky, I'm 45 and I only now lost my last grandparent. She was 95. Anyways, there is one part of my extended family on my dad's side, she was his mother, that is the epitome of evangelical, christofacist, MAGA shitheads. Anti-vaccine, anti-science, homophobic, transphobic, misogynistic, and racist as hell. Just some of the worst people imaginable. My aunt from that part is an Uber Karen that has no friends and just pisses everyone off whenever she's around. They're also grifters only there for any inheritance they might get.
After we put my grandmother into the ground, at the lunch that follows, if any of them so much as make eye contact with either me or my sister we're going to go off on them. I don't really care how the rest of the extended thinks of me and my folks will get over it. I said I'm happy to be that asshole who lays into this hateful MAGA fucks. After all this over, we will never see them ever again. Don't have any contact info for them, and don't care the rest of us are writing them off and cutting them out of our collective lives. Its going to feel so fucking good. Just have to get through the next few days. If you've read this far thank you. I've made my peace with her passing already and I am fine. Just go hug or hold the ones that are dearest to you, whether they have 2 or 4 legs. Edit at 11:24 pm MN time: Wow most viral, been reading the comments and I appreciate what most of you have said. I should have clarified that I won't be making a giant scene and get into a screaming match with those MAGA fuckwits. I am going to walk over to them, my uncle's wife in particular, and tell her she's a fascist cunt that everyone else hates. Then say that after I leave I hope I'll never see, hear, or otherwise think about her and her worthless shitbird children. I'll do this quietly and matter of factly. I have to get it out, I've been holding back for over 10 years. This will be cathartic. Maybe I'll make a post about the aftermath, I dont know yet. Also I just saw the stupid spelling error in the first meme. Fuck.
item.dc:creator: 5thAveSmugglerForHire
